task_completion.description: Create a multi-page diagram in draw.io with specific page names
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface should show at least two page tabs at the bottom, specifically named 'Journey Map' and 'Key Metrics'.

task_completion.description: Create a grid or swimlane structure on the 'Journey Map' page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Journey Map' page must display a clear grid or swimlane layout with 6 vertical columns (phases) and 6 horizontal rows (lanes).

task_completion.description: Label the phases and lanes correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: The vertical columns must be labeled: Research & Booking, Pre-Departure, Airport Departure, In-Flight, Arrival, Post-Trip. The horizontal rows must be labeled: Touchpoints, Channels, Actions (or Customer Actions), Emotions, Pain Points, Opportunities.

task_completion.description: Populate the journey map with content and flow indicators
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cells within the grid/swimlanes should contain text boxes, shapes, and flow arrows representing the passenger journey. Look for keywords like 'app', 'bag drop', 'security', 'hidden fees', or 'lost luggage' inside the diagram.

task_completion.description: Populate the 'Key Metrics' page with required lists
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Key Metrics' page must contain text or shapes explicitly listing the 'Top 5 Pain Points' and '3 Moments of Truth'.

task_completion.description: Save the diagram and export the Journey Map as a PNG
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, desktop view, or terminal must show the existence of 'passenger_journey_map.drawio' and 'passenger_journey_map.png' on the Desktop. The PNG should visually match the 'Journey Map' page.

integrity.description: Agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io application open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas, rather than just a terminal window running a script to generate XML.

integrity.description: Agent used actual diagramming elements instead of pasting raw text
integrity.visual_evidence: The journey map should use distinct shapes, swimlanes, or table cells for different pieces of content, not just a single large text box containing the copy-pasted requirements file.

integrity.description: The exported PNG is a genuine export of the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: If the PNG is opened or previewed, it should exactly match the visual appearance of the 'Journey Map' page created in draw.io, not a generic placeholder image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The requirements file is located at ~/Desktop/passenger_journey_requirements.txt. The diagram must have exactly two pages: 'Journey Map' and 'Key Metrics'. The 'Journey Map' page must have 6 vertical phases (Research & Booking, Pre-Departure, Airport Departure, In-Flight, Arrival, Post-Trip) and 6 horizontal lanes (Touchpoints, Channels, Actions/Customer Actions, Emotions, Pain Points, Opportunities). Look for content keywords in the diagram: 'hidden fees', 'bag drop', 'security', 'long queue', 'lost luggage', 'biometric', 'bag tracking', 'loyalty', 'app'. Output files must be saved to ~/Desktop/passenger_journey_map.drawio and ~/Desktop/passenger_journey_map.png.
